elements.title: Archibald John Davies
elements.contents: ... that stained glass artist Archibald John Davies ran a studio for 47 years at Bromsgrove Guild of Applied Arts, producing hundreds of works (example pictured)?

elements.title: Unexpectedly Naughty Fukami
elements.contents: ... that the author of Unexpectedly Naughty Fukami felt that the idea for the story was cliché when it was first suggested by her editors?

elements.title: a busy hospital in the capital of Malawi
elements.contents: ... that a busy hospital in the capital of Malawi handles 24,000 births per year by having ten babies in a bed and up to four in an incubator?

elements.title: Kathryn Johnson
elements.contents: ... that as the only woman reporter in her bureau, Kathryn Johnson was assigned to cover stories her male colleagues refused, such as the activities of Martin Luther King Jr.?

elements.title: Serious Sam II
elements.contents: ... that the publisher of Serious Sam II petitioned Guinness World Records to recognise it for the most enemies simultaneously displayed in a video game?

elements.title: Clara E. Thoms
elements.contents: ... that Clara E. Thoms toured the United States as a concert pianist at the age of eight?

elements.title: a solar eclipse
elements.contents: ... that two ancient Chinese astronomers are said to have been executed for failing to predict a solar eclipse because they were drinking?

elements.title: Vojin Đuričić
elements.contents: ... that Yugoslav finance minister Vojin Đuričić also headed an astronomical society and an aero club?

elements.title: SHeDAISY
elements.contents: ... that SHeDAISY's record label sent spatulas to radio programmers to encourage them to "flip" a poorly-received single?
